details: The scheme “Nirman Shramik Kalyan Yojana” was launched by the Labour and Employee's State Insurance Department, Government of Odisha. The objective of the scheme is to extend financial assistance to eligible building and other construction workers for continuing education of their wards/children under the “Nirman Shramik Kalyan Yojana” being extended by the Odisha Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.
benefits: Different benefits extended by Odisha Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board
For Class- 6th and 7th to girl child
20% additional benefit to girl child of beneficiary over and above normal assistance
Cash award to the children securing 90% or above in Class-10th
For Classes of B.A./B.Sc./ B.Com./PG Studies
For B. Tech/ MCA/ MBA/ B. Arch./ M. Arch./ M.Tech/ M. Sc./ B. Pharm/ M. Pharm/ Hotel Management & Catering Services/ Medical (includes Allopathic, Ayurvedic & Homeopathic) in Govt. Colleges only
For B.Ed / CT/ Nursing Training in Govt. Institutes
eligibility: The wards/children of the building and other construction workers are eligible to apply under the scheme.

The financial assistance is given to only the wards/children of such building and other construction (BoC) beneficiaries who have completed at least one year as registered beneficiaries under the Board. In case, both parents are eligible registered beneficiaries, assistance in respect of a child should not be extended to both parents, separately.
Financial assistance under the scheme is being extended to students from class 6th onwards.
A maximum of two children of a family are eligible to get the assistance.
For 6th and 7th standard the scheme is applicable only in respect of girl children; whereas for class 8th onwards the scheme is applicable to both girls and boys.
20% additional benefit over and above the normal educational assistance is being extended by the Board in respect of girl child from class 8th onwards.
The registered building and other construction beneficiaries should have made up-to-date annual contributions to the Board.
50 % annual attendance of the student in a class for that academic year is required for availing the said assistance.
The building and other construction beneficiary should have an active Bank Account with DBT enabled in his/her name.

online: Step 01:
For new users - Click on the Registration link on the Scholarship Portal i.e. (
https://scholarship.odisha.gov.in
), existing users can click on the ‘Login’ button.
Step 02:
Enter the Aadhaar number and proceed further.
Step 03:
Check your registered mobile or email to get the user ID and password.
Step 04:
Click on the login link at the portal and enter the User ID and password to apply for a scholarship.
Step 05:
Provide all necessary details like personal academic, eligibility, and bank information.
Step 06:
Click on the ‘Save as draft’ button to draft the information or click on the "Next" button to save and proceed with the scholarship form.
Step 07:
The preview of the completed form will appear on the page. Download the forms and take its print out.
Step 08:
The filled-in application will be processed for verification and disbursement by the concerned District Labour Officer in the e-Shramik Portal of the Board.
Step 09:

The educational assistance on being disbursed by the disbursing authority in the e-Shramik Portal of the Board, the same will automatically get updated in the State Scholarship Portal for information of all stakeholders.

documents_required: A copy of Aadhaar Card of the applicant
Mark Sheets/Certificate of last qualifying examination
Aadhaar Card of the parent registered with the OB&OCWW Board
Building and Other Construction ID Card
First page of the bank account of the registered building and other construction worker (parent) containing account number and IFSC

College ID card issued by the concerned authority, While in the absence of the same, the student may provide an Admission receipt/Library Card/Letter of Undertaking from the concerned college authority (Anyone).

faqs.question: What is the objective of the scheme?
faqs.answer: The objective of the scheme is to extend financial assistance to eligible building and other construction workers for continuing education of their wards/children under the “Nirman Shramik Kalyan Yojana” being extended by the Odisha Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.

faqs.question: Which is the implementing department of this scheme?
faqs.answer: Labour and Employee's State Insurance Department, Government of Odisha

faqs.question: How much amount of scholarship is paid under scheme?
faqs.answer: The scholarship under the scheme is given to the children from ₹2,000/- to ₹40,000/- per annum as per the eligibility criteria.

faqs.question: When will the students receive the scholarship?
faqs.answer: The students will receive the scholarship on completion of 1st year of course.

faqs.question: How much is the additional benefit given to the girl child under the scheme?
faqs.answer: 20% additional benefit to girl child of beneficiary over and above normal assistance.

faqs.question: Who is eligible to apply under the scheme?
faqs.answer: The wards/children of building and other construction workers are eligible to apply under the scheme.

faqs.question: Students of which classes can apply for this scheme?
faqs.answer: The students who are studying in Class 6th to Post-graduation can apply under the scheme.

faqs.question: How many children in a family can avail of the benefit of the scheme?
faqs.answer: A maximum of two children of a family are eligible to get the assistance.

faqs.question: Can both boys & girls get assistance under the scheme?
faqs.answer: For 6th and 7th standard the scheme is applicable only in respect of girl children; whereas for class 8th onwards the scheme is applicable to both girls and boys.

faqs.question: How to apply under the scheme?
faqs.answer: The eligible candidate can apply for the scheme through Odisha State Scholarship Portal i.e. (https://scholarship.odisha.gov.in).
